profile.name: Dylan Borland
profile.summary: Former USAF 1N1 GEOINT specialist who observed a 100-foot silent equilateral triangle craft at close range near the NASA hangar at Langley AFB in summer 2012. He pursued official whistleblower channels - filing with the ICIG in March 2023 and testifying to AARO - before providing sworn testimony before the House Oversight Committee in September 2025. Borland claims sustained retaliation including security clearance manipulation and blacklisting from intelligence community work.
early_career: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force in 2010; assigned to Langley AFB, Virginia as a 1N1 GEOINT (Geospatial Intelligence) specialist
Conducted 24-hour ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance) operations in support of Special Operations Forces
Following honorable discharge in 2013, transitioned to defense contracting, analyzing video, radar, and electro-optical imagery for BAE Systems and Intrepid Solutions

key_events.event: Enlisted in the U.S. Air Force; assigned to Langley AFB as a 1N1 GEOINT specialist supporting Special Operations Forces ISR missions.

At approximately 0130 hours, observed a silent equilateral triangle craft approximately 100 feet in diameter emerge near the NASA hangar at Langley AFB. The craft approached within 100 feet, hovered for several minutes, caused phone interference, produced no kinetic disturbance, then rapidly ascended and departed at high speed.
Honorably discharged from the U.S. Air Force; began career in defense contracting as a senior imagery and intelligence analyst.
Formally initiated federal whistleblower process by reporting his sighting and alleged retaliation to the Intelligence Community Inspector General (ICIG).
Testified before AARO through official whistleblower channels, providing a formal account of the 2012 Langley sighting and alleged subsequent retaliation.

Testified under oath before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform at the hearing titled 'Restoring Public Trust Through UAP Transparency and Whistleblower Protection,' describing the Langley triangle encounter and claims of sustained retaliation.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.role: Former NRO/UAPTF intelligence officer, UAP whistleblower
associated_people.relationship: Both testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee during the September 2025 UAP transparency and whistleblower protection hearing.

associated_people.name: Jay Stratton
associated_people.relationship: Borland reported his sighting and whistleblower complaint to AARO during Stratton's tenure as director, as part of the official federal whistleblower process.

associated_people.name: Jeremy Corbell
associated_people.relationship: Interviewed Borland on the Weaponized podcast (Episodes 90-91, late 2025), producing the 'The Reluctant UFO Whistleblower' episodes that brought his account to broad public attention.

disclosures.title: House UAP Transparency Hearing
disclosures.outlet: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
disclosures.notes: Testified under oath on September 9, 2025 at the hearing 'Restoring Public Trust Through UAP Transparency and Whistleblower Protection.' Described the summer 2012 close-range triangle UAP encounter at Langley AFB and detailed alleged retaliation including security clearance manipulation and blacklisting from intelligence community employment.

disclosures.title: The Reluctant UFO Whistleblower
disclosures.notes: Two-part appearance on the Weaponized podcast (Episodes 90-91). Provided an extended first-person account of the Langley AFB sighting, the whistleblower process through ICIG and AARO, and the alleged retaliation he experienced in the defense contracting sector.

sources.title: House Oversight Committee - Borland Written Testimony (Sep 9, 2025)

sources.notes: NewsNation coverage of Borland's whistleblower account and claims of retaliation.

career_connections.relationship: Borland followed the same official whistleblower path established by Grusch - ICIG filing, AARO testimony, then congressional testimony
Both are connected through the AARO institutional context; Borland testified to AARO, which Stratton had briefly directed before Kirkpatrick
